expectations.text: Game title correctly identified as LUTA: Luminoria Tactics or Luminoria Tactics
expectations.evidence: Document header and Product Identity table both list 'LUTA: Luminoria Tactics' exactly.

expectations.text: Genre classified as mid-core or tactical/strategy with session length benchmark referenced (15–30 min)
expectations.evidence: Genre is correctly 'Tactical / Strategy RPG', but session length is listed as '10–30 min' (line 57), not the 15–30 min mid-core benchmark. Lower bound is off by 5 minutes.

expectations.text: Mid-core D1 retention benchmark referenced (25–35%), NOT the casual range (35–45%)
expectations.evidence: KPI table (line 160) lists Day-1 Retention as '35–45%' labelled 'Benchmark for tactical RPG genre' — this is the casual game range, not the correct mid-core range of 25–35%.

expectations.text: Core loop is written using the action→reward→progression→loop structure, even if flagged as assumption
expectations.evidence: No core loop section exists in the document. Section 11 lists 'What is the core game loop?' as an open question for the studio, meaning it was never drafted.

expectations.text: ROAS targets include a time window (D7, D30, or D90) — not just 'good ROAS'
expectations.evidence: No ROAS targets appear anywhere in the document. The KPI table covers installs, rating, retention, and IAP conversion only — ROAS is entirely absent.

expectations.text: iOS measurement section mentions ATT, SKAdNetwork, or the ~25–35% opt-in rate limitation
expectations.evidence: The document is scoped entirely to Android/Google Play. There is no iOS measurement section and no mention of ATT, SKAdNetwork, or opt-in rates anywhere.

expectations.text: UA channels do not list IronSource as a standalone network or Vungle (outdated names)
expectations.evidence: Paid UA channels listed are 'Google UAC (primary), Meta Ads (secondary), TikTok Ads' (line 123). IronSource and Vungle do not appear anywhere in the document.

expectations.text: All unknown KPIs are marked [NEEDS INPUT] or [ASSUMPTION] — not fabricated as facts
expectations.evidence: KPI table states D1 Retention '35–45%' and D7 Retention '15–25%' as plain benchmark figures with no [NEEDS INPUT] or [ASSUMPTION] tags. These are unknown for this specific game and should be flagged. Other fields use informal qualifiers like 'confirm with studio' but the KPI table itself contains no structured uncertainty markers.
